description: Baseline zsh-bench direct because hey zbench flag forwarding rejects --iters

asi.note: hey zbench --iters 4 failed due Nushell unknown flag; direct zsh-bench used for exploratory baseline

description: Simplify antidote static health check and remove duplicate bun completion source; also tried Nu --wrapped flag forwarding

asi.decision: zshrc change not kept: first_prompt and first_command regressed/noise; command_lag improved but not enough alone. Harness --wrapped did not fix hey zbench --iters in current invocation.

description: Remove duplicate unconditional bun completion source only

asi.decision: Discarded: duplicate bun completion source removal did not improve primary or first_command in this run.

description: Simplify _cache to avoid command mtime checks for cached command-generated snippets

asi.decision: Discarded: primary and first_command lag regressed; input_lag improved slightly but not enough.

description: Defer fzf shell integration via antidote kind:defer

asi.decision: Discarded for now: first_command/command/input improved somewhat but primary first_prompt lag regressed vs baseline; fzf keybinding behavior may also become delayed. Cache bundle regenerated during experiment and should be restored.

description: Fix repo zbench harness to forward flags with Nushell def --wrapped [...args]; validated with ./bin/hey zbench --iters 4

asi.note: Shell performance roughly noise vs direct baseline; kept because it fixes requested exploratory command. Installed hey on PATH still points to previous generation until rebuild, so used ./bin/hey for validation.

description: Undefer zsh-vi-mode to test whether deferred loading causes high first_command_lag

asi.decision: Discarded: first_prompt and especially first_command lag regressed substantially. Hypothesis disproven; keep zsh-vi-mode deferred. zbench-report tail omitted exit_time_ms in run_experiment output, logged 0 placeholder.

description: Delay heavy deferred plugins (zsh-vi-mode, atuin, autopair) by 1s with antidote defer-options

asi.decision: Discarded: command/input lag improved, but first_prompt_lag regressed vs baseline and first_command stayed about the same. This also delays expected interactive features by a full second, so not worth keeping.

description: Limit powerlevel10k antidote bundle to powerlevel10k.zsh-theme instead of sourcing both powerlevel10k and powerlevel9k theme files

asi.decision: Discarded for now: first_command improved, but first_prompt was worse than best/baseline and command_lag regressed slightly. Need confirmation if revisited because this may be a real behavior-preserving cleanup but current primary metric did not improve.

description: Remove manual background autopair-init block because zsh-autopair auto-initializes when its deferred source runs

asi.decision: Discarded: expected to remove redundant background work, but first_prompt, first_command, and command_lag all regressed. Keep current explicit background block for now.

description: Remove duplicate fpath append for zsh completions from completion.zsh because .zshrc already prepends the same directory before compinit

asi.decision: Discarded: duplicate fpath cleanup preserved behavior in principle, but primary first_prompt_lag regressed and no secondary metric improved meaningfully versus baseline.

description: Source zcompiled Antidote static bundle when .zsh_plugins.zsh.zwc is newer than static bundle

asi.decision: Discarded: using a zcompiled static bundle regressed first_prompt and first_command lag versus baseline. Not worth adding extra cache invalidation complexity.

description: Always use compinit -C with zcompdump cache instead of daily full compinit -i refresh

asi.decision: Discarded: removing the daily refresh/check path regressed first_prompt_lag and did not materially improve first_command/input. Also weakens completion self-healing, so not worth keeping.

description: Remove deferred zsh-vi-mode plugin to test if it is responsible for first_command_lag and command_lag

asi.decision: Discarded: removing zsh-vi-mode regressed first_prompt, first_command, and command_lag substantially. This suggests zsh-vi-mode is not the cause of measured command lag; keep it deferred to preserve behavior.

description: Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt in custom prompt config by setting POWERLEVEL9K_INSTANT_PROMPT=quiet

asi.decision: Discarded: despite existing instant-prompt sourcing block, enabling it in the custom prompt config regressed first_prompt, first_command, and command_lag. Keep instant prompt off for this prompt.

description: Stop sourcing generated .p10k.zsh before extra.zshrc because active prompt.zsh is loaded later and reloads p10k

asi.decision: Discarded: plausible duplicate prompt config cleanup, but first_prompt, first_command, and command_lag all regressed versus baseline. Keep current .p10k.zsh source order until a better prompt-loading redesign is tested.

description: Remove deferred atuin plugin to test if atuin shell integration contributes to early command lag

asi.decision: Discarded: removing atuin did not improve first_prompt versus the best baseline and first_command/command_lag stayed in the same range. It also disables expected history integration, so not worth keeping.

description: Delay heavy deferred plugins by 0.2s with antidote defer-options to reduce first-command contention without visibly delaying features

asi.decision: Discarded: input_lag improved, but first_prompt and especially first_command lag regressed substantially. Short delay appears to push plugin work into the benchmark's first-command window rather than avoiding it.

description: Remove fzf shell integration entirely to diagnose whether fzf completion/keybindings contribute to startup or command lag

asi.decision: Discarded: removing fzf did not improve primary first_prompt_lag or secondary command metrics enough, and it disables expected Ctrl-T/Alt-C/fzf integration. Keep fzf loaded normally.

description: Remove deferred fast-syntax-highlighting to diagnose whether syntax highlighting contributes to input/command lag

asi.decision: Discarded: input_lag improved materially, but removing syntax highlighting is a visible feature loss and primary first_prompt_lag did not beat baseline. First_command/command lag remained in the same range, so not worth keeping.

description: Remove deferred zsh-autosuggestions to diagnose whether autosuggestion widget work contributes to command/input lag

asi.decision: Discarded: command_lag improved, but primary first_prompt did not beat the best baseline and removing autosuggestions is a visible feature loss. Keep autosuggestions deferred.

description: Remove deferred zsh-history-substring-search to diagnose whether history substring widgets contribute to command/input lag

asi.decision: Discarded: first_command improved slightly, but primary first_prompt_lag regressed and removing history substring search breaks expected Up/Down substring history behavior configured in keybinds.zsh.

description: Switch zsh-autosuggestions strategy from completion to history to reduce autosuggestion work while preserving suggestions

asi.decision: Kept provisionally: this is the first run beating primary baseline and improves first_command/command/input versus baseline while preserving autosuggestions, though semantics change from completion-based suggestions to history-based suggestions. Needs confirmation rerun before finalizing.

description: Confirmation rerun for autosuggestions history strategy using repo ZDOTDIR explicitly

asi.decision: Discarded confirmation: the previous history-strategy win did not reproduce when benchmarking with repo ZDOTDIR explicitly. Primary first_prompt_lag regressed versus baseline and first_command returned to baseline range, so do not keep this semantics-changing autosuggestion strategy change based on one noisy run.

description: Set ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_MANUAL_REBIND=1 while keeping completion strategy to avoid autosuggestions rebinding widgets on every precmd

asi.decision: Discarded: first_command and command_lag improved, but primary first_prompt_lag regressed versus baseline. Behavior risk is moderate because manual rebind can miss widgets added later, so not worth keeping without primary improvement.

description: Lower ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_BUFFER_MAX_SIZE from 30 to 15 while keeping completion strategy

asi.decision: Discarded: smaller autosuggestion buffer did not improve primary or first_command lag, despite modest command_lag improvement. It also reduces suggestions for moderately long commands.

description: Baseline rerun before hook-focused profiling

asi.note: precmd inventory included zsh-jj precmd_vcs_info; manual timing found it dominates command_lag.

description: Remove unused rkh/zsh-jj plugin; active prompt uses custom async jj segment

asi.decision: Kept: removes precmd_vcs_info from prompt loop. command_lag improved from ~204ms to 40-51ms across confirmation runs.

description: Add Antidote static bundle invalidation when .zsh_plugins.txt is newer

asi.decision: Kept: preserves behavior of plugin-list edits and keeps zsh-jj removal effective without manual cache deletion; benchmark remains in improved command_lag band.
